issues.title: SLA policy will be violating in 3 minutes
issues.description:
Konnect Insights [https://app.konnectinsights.com/emails/images/logo.png]
https://app.konnectinsights.com/

SLA Policy: FRT-15Mins

SLA Description: Tracking all the first response and Every Response to be done
within defined timeline



4011650577 Re: AI Classification Data Validation    04 Jun 2026 03:39 PM
[https://app.konnectinsights.com/ticketingview?tid=4011650577] Hi Divya, Thank
you for sharing the thread. I have checked with the technical team, and
parent-level AI classifications are currently active for Pulsar and other
groups. I have also shared the descriptions received from Dhwanika with the team
to begin the same training for the Chetak group. Once this is complete, I will
share the file with you for verification. On Thu, Jun 4, 2026 at 3:14 PM Divya
Pathela wrote: > AI Setup Discussions in our current pack > > Thank you & Best
regards, > Divya Pathela > *Chief ORM and Marketing Manager* > Yugshree
Innovations Pvt. Ltd > > > ---------- Forwarded message --------- > From: Divya
Pathela > Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 at 18:00 > Subject: Fwd: AI Classification Data
Validation > To: Dhwanika Dhanak , Social Escalation < >
socialescalation@yugshree.com> > > > > Thank you & Best regards, > Divya Pathela
> *Chief ORM and Marketing Manager* > Yugshree Innovations Pvt. Ltd > > >
---------- Forwarded message --------- > From: Yash Chauhan > Date: Wed, 22 Apr
2026 at 17:59 > Subject: Re: AI Classification Data Validation > To: Divya
Pathela > > > Hi Divya, > > Please review the results for the attached data. > >
Regards, > Yash > > On Mon, Apr 13, 2026 at 5:53 PM Yash Chauhan > wrote: > >>
Hi Divya, >> >> Re-sharing the data again, please check. >> >> >> >> On Mon, Apr
13, 2026 at 1:00 PM Yash Chauhan >> wrote: >> >>> Hi Divya, >>> Please check the
updated prompt based on the feedback, kindly check if >>> anything is missing or
incorrect here: >>> >>> TASK: >>> Classify the given text into ONE category. >>>
>>> OUTPUT RULES: >>> - Output ONLY ONE category name >>> - NO explanation >>> -
NO extra words >>> - If not related, output: Irrelevant >>> >>>
---------------------------------------- >>> >>> STEP 1: CHECK ABUSE (HIGHEST
PRIORITY) >>> >>> Abuse >>> → Direct gaali, hate, or insult targeting
Bajaj/product/person >>> >>> Abuse trolling >>> → Sarcasm, mocking, taunting,
fan wars, exaggerated negativity >>> >>> Spam Irrelevant >>> → Random,
meaningless, or abuse NOT related to Bajaj >>> >>>
---------------------------------------- >>> >>> STEP 2: CHECK RELEVANCE >>> >>>
If not related to Bajaj → Irrelevant >>> >>>
---------------------------------------- >>> >>> STEP 3: CLASSIFY INTENT >>> >>>
PRODUCT RELATED: >>> >>> Product >>> → Specific issues (engine, mileage,
vibration, defects, parts) >>> >>> Product Vehicle Performance >>> → Speed,
pickup, mileage, riding performance >>> >>> Product Performance and Reliability
>>> → Long-term issues, repeated failures >>> >>> Product Appreciation >>> →
Praise for performance, power, design >>> >>> Design Comfort Build Quality >>> →
Comfort, seat, suspension, styling, materials >>> >>> >>> >>> OWNERSHIP /
CONTENT: >>> >>> Ownership Review >>> → Long-term experience (positive or
negative) >>> >>> UGC (user-generated content) >>> → Ride posts, delivery,
trips, modifications, events >>> >>> >>> >>> SERVICE: >>> >>> Service >>> →
Negative service experience, cost, delay >>> >>> Service (Positive) >>> →
Positive service feedback >>> >>> Service and Maintenance Information >>> →
Informational / how-to / fixes >>> >>> >>> >>> PURCHASE / COMPARISON: >>> >>>
Comparison with other bikes >>> → Comparing with competitors >>> >>> Sales
Booking Delivery >>> → Booking, delivery, showroom issues >>> >>> >>> >>> EV:
>>> >>> Charging EV Ecosystem >>> → Charging, battery, infrastructure issues >>>
>>> >>> >>> BRAND / MARKETING: >>> >>> Brand/ External Comments >>> → General
brand comments (no specific issue) >>> >>> Marketing campaign feedback >>> →
Ads, campaigns >>> >>> Rider community feedback >>> → Community-level feedback
>>> >>> >>> >>> QUERIES: >>> >>> User Requests and Feedbacks >>> → Asking price,
features, availability, suggestions >>> >>> Queries General Information >>> →
Gen... Ticket Id: 4011650577

BNY Mellon is a global investments company dedicated to helping its clients manage and service their financial assets throughout the investment lifecycle. Whether providing financial services for institutions, corporations or individual investors Jobs at BNY Mellon Careers Opportunities for Graduate Entry Level Fresher role For over 235 years, Bank of New York Mellon (BNY Mellon) has been at the center of the global financial markets, providing the world’s leading institutions the tools, capabilities, and services to be distinctive investors. BNY Early Career Opportunities | Explore our Programs 2025 Explore BNY Early Career Programs — from global internships and analyst roles to U.S. pre-internships and U.K. apprenticeships. Start your journey with a global leader at the center of the world’s financial ecosystem. Shape your career path with BNY Early Career Programs. Our early career programs offer on-the-job experiences that build technical and professional skills while opening doors to global opportunities. Pre-Internship Virtual experience designed for U.S. undergrad freshmen and sophomores, this virtual program offers an early introduction to financial services. Our virtual pre-internship programs offer U.S. undergrad freshmen and sophmores an early introduction to financial services. FRESHMAN JUMPSTART A virtual summer experience consisting of two half-day sessions over two weeks, designed to introduce first-year undergraduate students to the financial services industry. Internship 8-10 week internship offering real-world experience across core areas of financial services. Our 8-10 week internship program offers real-world experience and global exposure to undergraduate students across core areas of financial services. Would you like us to notify you when our 2026 programs open for applications on August 5, 2025? Sign up and join our talent network below. Analyst Program EARLY CAREER OPPORTUNITIES Our 24-month analyst program offers real-world experience and global exposure for undergraduate students across core areas of financial services. Apprenticeship 4-year full-time program designed for students in London and Manchester, offering on-the-job experience alongside a degree or professional qualification funded by BNY. For more details information BNY Early Graduate Careers, Click here!
Keep reading on LinkedIn

tickets.subject: GoodWe Hosts Pakistani Consul General in Shanghai to Deepen Renewable Energy Partnership

thread.body:
GoodWe News Update

By GoodWe


By GoodWe

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/goodwe-hosts-pakistani-consul-general-shanghai-deepen-zrive?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BM1%2FmPkiKS3KHWp%2Fceztzkg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=1nH5QXnzrl8HU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~mdwk1scg~y1-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdwk1scg-y1&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWU1YjM4N2NkZDk0MTliYTk4ZjYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmI2NTdlN2NjZmE3Y2EyYTNhZjI0MzNjMDViOWQyOWEwOWRmZDBlMzQ5YzAxZDkxYiwxLDE%3D
  
As part of its continued commitment to international clean energy cooperation, GoodWe hosted Mr. Shehzad Ahmad Khan, Consul General of Pakistan in Shanghai, and his delegation at its global headquarters in Suzhou. This visit marked a key milestone in advancing strategic collaboration between China and Pakistan in the renewable energy sector. GoodWe’s Vice President, Alex Pan, led the team in welcoming the delegation. During his visit, the Consul General noted that GoodWe is already a familiar name in Pakistan’s energy sector. Visiting the company’s headquarters, he remarked, gave him “a much clearer and more personal impression of the people, technologies, and values behind the brand.” He also expressed that this exchange is a promising step towards deepening future cooperation, particularly in light of Pakistan’s ongoing energy transition. This year also marks the 10th anniversary of GoodWe’s presence in Pakistan, a testament to the company’s long-standing dedication to the local market. James Hou, General Manager of GoodWe South Asia (SA) and Southeast Asia (SEA), reaffirmed the company’s long-standing commitment to the Pakistani market. He highlighted GoodWe’s established local presence, including dedicated sales and service centers, market-driven product innovations, and real-world installation cases across residential, commercial and industrial, and utility sectors. “This reflects GoodWe’s vision of driving the world’s smart energy future through open, win-win partnerships,” said James Hou. “And we’re proud to support Pakistan’s clean energy ambitions with reliable technologies, dependable service network, and long-term commitment.” To meet the evolving energy needs of the market, GoodWe has introduced a lineup of innovative and tailored solutions to the Pakistani market, including high-efficiency residential on-grid inverters known for their compact design and whisper-noise operation, low-voltage energy storage systems tailored for the growing demand for household self-consumption, as well as flexible solutions for large-scale projects.
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/goodwe-hosts-pakistani-consul-general-shanghai-deepen-zrive?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BM1%2FmPkiKS3KHWp%2Fceztzkg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=1nH5QXnzrl8HU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-bze0lp~mdwk1scg~y1-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdwk1scg-y1&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWU1YjM4N2NkZDk0MTliYTk4ZjYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmI2NTdlN2NjZmE3Y2EyYTNhZjI0MzNjMDViOWQyOWEwOWRmZDBlMzQ5YzAxZDkxYiwxLDE%3D
  
      

----------------------------------------

tickets.subject: Who’s feeling the pain of Trump’s tariffs?

thread.body:
The Economist’s week ahead

By The Economist


By The Economist

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/whos-feeling-pain-trumps-tariffs-the-economist-4odte?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3B%2F%2FWUdCSlRiOofYYTmz0Pxg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=0RyQ8rpGYY9bU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~mdwwwqvy~dg-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdwwwqvy-dg&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWU1YmQ4NmM3ZDc0OTliYTg4ZTYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmJmNjBiMWU4ODU1NGJlZGMwMWU4NWMzY2VhNmFjYWYzZWRlOWM2NmZhNjY5Mjc2OCwxLDE%3D
  
Enjoying this newsletter? Get it in your inbox every weekend, as well as our daily round-up of The Economist’s best journalism, by signing up for free here. Hello from London, This time of year often brings changes of routine. For one thing, I’ve taken over this newsletter for a couple of weekends, while Adam Roberts takes a richly deserved holiday. Even more importantly (sorry, Adam), this is the week of our special summer issue, which contains four terrific long reads from our colleagues at 1843. The quartet is led by Liam Taylor’s story of the horrific ordeal of a group of gold-miners in South Africa who were trapped underground for months. You can also read about a strange case of stolen identity, the hopefuls striving to pass the world’s hardest exam and a young Israeli who spent 500 days as a hostage of Hamas. One place where I suspect there’ll be little time to relax this summer is at LVMH. Not long ago the world’s biggest luxury-goods group was riding high—valuable enough to make its boss, Bernard Arnault, briefly the world’s richest man. As we report this weekend, times are harder now. That’s partly down to bad luck, but also to troubles of LVMH’s own making. Donald Trump’s tariffs, of course, won’t be helping. The deal Mr Trump struck with the European Union at the weekend means those won’t be as high as they might have been—but a 15% levy on most goods is still a big jump from the pre-Trump world. Our analysis suggests that so far foreign companies are swallowing a portion of Mr Trump’s tariffs, in the form of lower profits, rather than passing them on in full to American consumers. But sooner or later the American economy will feel the effects of Mr Trump’s folly. As well as a more muscular (but misguided) approach to trade, the Trump administration is taking a more muscular (but not so nutty) approach to space. Space Command, set up during Mr Trump’s first term, used to be wary of talking about its offensive capabilities. Now, as our defence editor reports, it embraces the idea. Patrick Lane, Senior digital editor  Recommended reads Who’s feeling the pain of Trump’s tariffs?  Last year America charged levies averaging 2% on its imports of goods. Following the launch of President Donald Trump’s trade war, it now has an “effective” tariff of over 16%, the highest since the 1930s. And rates could go even higher. Mr Trump has written strongly worded letters to many of America’s biggest trading partners threatening further levies on August 1st. Can Bernard Arnault steer LVMH out of crisis? Louis Vuitton’s new 17,000-square-foot development in Shanghai is, quite literally, the luxury brand’s Chinese flagship. The structure, which serves as a store, restaurant, museum and billboard, is shaped like a giant boat, its hull emblazoned with Louis Vuitton’s unmistakable monogram print. To some, it is also a metaphor for Louis Vuitton’s parent company, LVMH, which is floundering in China and beyond. Is it a superyacht headed for promising new waters, asks Flavio Cereda-Parin of GAM, an asset manager, or “Titanic 2.0”? Do probiotics work? A dazzling menagerie of microbes live inside the human gut—by some counts a few thousand different species. Most residents of this gut microbiome are not the disease-causing kind. In fact, many do useful jobs, such as breaking down certain carbohydrates, fibres and proteins that the human body would otherwise struggle to digest. We look at which drinks and foods intended to boost the gut's good microbes really do what they purport to. Most read by subscribers The economics of superintelligence What if AI made the world’s economic growth explode? A little poetic justice for Donald Trump Go beyond the headlines Get unlimited digital access to The Economist by subscribing today.

thread.body:
Current Careers Opportunities 

By Career For Freshers


By Career For Freshers

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/microsoft-careers-opportunities-work-from-home-entry-krvmf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BUCI26lpQRJq1QdWbY97FBA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=3dyyWaiFPdaHU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~mdykx92i~w4-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdykx92i-w4&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjQ4ZmM2ZDI0MzllYTg4YTYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmIwNGJjZGVmZDY3ODlhZjBjMDZlODdhZjcxYzY2NDFjZjJmZDlhMDJmNTFkMjM2OCwxLDE%3D
  
Microsoft Corporation is an American multinational corporation and technology company headquartered in Redmond, Washington. Microsoft’s best-known software products are the Windows line of operating systems, the Microsoft 365 suite of productivity applications, and the Edge web browser. Microsoft Work from Home Opportunities 2025 Microsoft Careers Opportunities for Graduate Fresher, Entry Level, Mid Level to Experience Professionals in various domain such as Technology, Engineering, Sales, Marketing, Sales, Digital, Cyber, AI, Robotics and many more Microsoft is a company where passionate innovators come to collaborate, envision what can be and take their careers further. This is a world of more possibilities, more innovation, more openness, and the sky is the limit thinking in a cloud-enabled world. This opportunity will allow you to bring your aspirations, talent, potential—and excitement for the journey ahead. Microsoft Careers Opportunities for Work from Home 2025 This role is flexible in that you can work up to 100% from home. Software Engineer – Fullstack, Multiple Locations As a  Software Engineer you will develop software, tools and code to be used in support of design, infrastructure, and technology platforms as well as commercial or end-user applications. This opportunity will allow you to create and implement extensible and maintainable code for a product, service, or feature. You will partner with stakeholders to determine user requirements for a feature and consider a variety of feedback channels to incorporate insights into future designs or solution fixes. Qualifications: Bachelor’s Degree (or currently pursuing a degree) in Computer Science or related technical discipline, OR equivalent experience. Proven experience coding in languages including, but not limited to, C, C++, C#, Java, JavaScript, or Python. Software Engineer – Security, Multiple Locations As a Software Engineer focused on security you will develop software, tools and code to be used in support of design, infrastructure, and technology platforms as well as commercial or end-user applications. You will help ensure the software, tools, and code meets the highest standards for security. Software Engineer – Systems, Multiple Locations As a  Software Engineer in Operating Systems, you will develop systems software, frameworks, and platform level code used in support of hardware. You will contribute to Operating Systems like Windows and Azure Linux and participate in design and development to power Azure infrastructure in the cloud, Edge, technology platforms for commercial, and/or end-user applications. For more details information to apply Microsoft Graduate Careers, Click here!
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/microsoft-careers-opportunities-work-from-home-entry-krvmf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BUCI26lpQRJq1QdWbY97FBA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=3dyyWaiFPdaHU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-bze0lp~mdykx92i~w4-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdykx92i-w4&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjQ4ZmM2ZDI0MzllYTg4YTYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmIwNGJjZGVmZDY3ODlhZjBjMDZlODdhZjcxYzY2NDFjZjJmZDlhMDJmNTFkMjM2OCwxLDE%3D

tickets.subject: Norway Becomes OpenAI’s Power Source

thread.body:
----------
## **This Week in AI: **

_No jargon, no filler—just the biggest AI developments worth knowing right now. Perfect for quick industry insights, so you can skip the buzzwords and get straight to the good stuff. Let’s dive into this week’s AI shake-ups, just as promised:_

OpenAI just revealed ChatGPT is on track to hit **700 million weekly users**, a number that rivals TikTok and crushes almost every SaaS product on Earth. Meanwhile, Grok made its play in the AI video space with a bold claim: it’s better than Sora, Veo, and Midjourney combined. And OpenAI’s infrastructure play? It just got a major upgrade, with a new data center project in Norway powering its Stargate ambitions.

Let’s get into it.

**In This Issue:**

* **ChatGPT on Pace for 700M Weekly Users** → OpenAI claims ChatGPT is becoming a mass product—fast. [(link)](https://techcrunch.com/2025/08/04/openai-says-chatgpt-is-on-track-to-reach-700m-weekly-users/)

* **Grok’s New Video Model Sparks Debate** → xAI enters the AI video arms race with some _very_ big comparisons. [(link)](https://mashable.com/article/grok-imagine-compared-veo-3-sora-midjourney)

**OpenAI Breaks Ground on Stargate in Norway** → A massive data center and

* supercomputing hub to fuel future AI models. [(link)](https://openai.com/index/introducing-stargate-norway/)

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/aaddd400-99c3-4d04-b65e-5aa2a60425b8/image.png?t=1754369522)
Follow image link: (https://techcrunch.com/2025/08/04/openai-says-chatgpt-is-on-track-to-reach-700m-weekly-users/)
Caption: 

### 🟣 [ChatGPT on Pace for 700M Weekly Users](https://techcrunch.com/2025/08/04/openai-says-chatgpt-is-on-track-to-reach-700m-weekly-users/)

**TL;DR:**

OpenAI confirmed that ChatGPT is on pace to hit 700 million weekly active users. That figure includes all tiers, free, Plus, and enterprise, and signals massive mainstream adoption. For comparison, that’s more weekly usage than Pinterest, X, and even Zoom.

**Our Take:**

OpenAI is closing in on Google-level usage metrics. If this trajectory holds, ChatGPT won’t just be a chatbot, it’ll be a platform. The challenge now is productization: can they turn this user base into a sustainable utility beyond novelty?

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/fee9fb6a-b1fb-4ac2-bd8e-461ad00a5aab/image.png?t=1754369572)
Follow image link: (https://mashable.com/article/grok-imagine-compared-veo-3-sora-midjourney)
Caption: 

### 🟣 [Grok’s New Video Model Sparks Debate](https://mashable.com/article/grok-imagine-compared-veo-3-sora-midjourney)

**TL;DR:**

xAI just launched Grok Imagine, a multimodal video generation model that Elon Musk’s team claims outperforms OpenAI’s Sora, Google’s Veo 3, and Midjourney’s latest release. Grok Imagine outputs clips from text prompts, focusing on realism, speed, and flexibility.

**Our Take:**

xAI is swinging big. Whether it actually beats Sora or Veo depends on benchmarks we haven’t seen yet. But if true, it could cement xAI as more than a meme-fueled LLM project. The space is crowded, but Grok’s reputation gives it a platform to make noise.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/0602ed65-14cb-4ebf-84f9-b453fe9f2099/image.png?t=1754369610)
Follow image link: (https://openai.com/index/introducing-stargate-norway/)
Caption: 

### 🟣 [OpenAI Breaks Ground on Stargate in Norway](https://openai.com/index/introducing-stargate-norway/)

**TL;DR:**

OpenAI announced that construction has started on Stargate Norway, a massive new data center powered by renewable hydroelectric energy. The site will serve as a key infrastructure node for training and deploying future frontier models.

**Our Take:**

This is about control. By owning the compute stack, OpenAI can run faster and with fewer dependencies. Norway’s cheap energy, political stability, and cool climate make it an ideal choice. Watch this space: future model breakthroughs will likely come from facilities like Stargate.




----------### 🚀 **Thank you for reading The Download**

_Your trusted source for__ the latest AI developments to keep you in the loop, but never overwhelmed. _🙂_ _

_*For sponsorship opportunities, email ___sponsor@thedownload.ai__


———

In the meantime, if you have any questions or feedback for us, please reach out to us here.https://www.linkedin.com/comm/e/v2?urlhash=_4og&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Elinkedin%2Ecom%2Fhelp%2Flinkedin%2Fask%2FSP-LISPQ&lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_marketplace_provider_new_lead_04%3BO%2F%2BlTr%2F3QsWt0b0jZaHysw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=2mh1l7ieZ0bbU1&trk=eml-email_marketplace_provider_new_lead_04-hero-0-contact_us&trkEmail=eml-email_marketplace_provider_new_lead_04-hero-0-contact_us-null-bze0lp~mdyy6wci~dg-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdyy6wci-dg

tickets.subject: Hello Developer: Apple UI design kits are here.

thread.body:
AUGUST 2025

Hello Developer

A collage showing screens with new features announced at WWDC25, including the new design.

Design your future

Apple UI design kits for Figma and Sketch are now available for iOS and iPadOS 26.

Download now
https://c.apple.com/r?v=2&a=JO1XYJwWmbNCvWbplzNuaJUQ%2ByQKjhkfld3wlX%2FzieTz7ol5kfxe7GYHndPSJ79pcKpwcYGrgXT2YeOdWRybMAmKp0wP98EeEQNjqdKJ3BdDgZ6HePWNiUOQ9HmymUXm6Vh8kah3Tr5K14qWtyAk3xFs5aWd9Dhq0fGghdOSdAHNIACZ6%2B7QX%2Fz0%2FyHX%2FMZxwe4MoCh9jiFWC7py1zt%2FNBIgs6%2BjoVGMedsCj3i%2FdCL4QX9EEHjPG65a3f1IOBBhi1tG6M4zhncmRFeHNNEMYLG8VbYTNBlhV5CmHS%2FPztRXXxjdX%2Faf4aK5Xm%2BtUH%2FaAuIYYu7ZkocLbrX3G4HtulJidsDuRMU%2FRGpx7MWyNjVayLgffYr8R3djFe8Qir4c

A woman stands on stage in a darkened auditorium in front of an audience. Behind her is a screen showing illustrations of the new Apple design system, including clear buttons and sliders. The screen has a light-gray background with a grid.

Watch a deep dive into WWDC25

Learn more about this year‘s new tools and technologies in this all-new session recorded live in Cupertino.

Watch now
https://c.apple.com/r?v=2&a=JO1XYJwWmbNCvWbplzNuaJUQ%2ByQKjhkfld3wlX%2FzieTz7ol5kfxe7GYHndPSJ79pcKpwcYGrgXT2YeOdWRybMAmKp0wP98EeEQNjqdKJ3BdDgZ6HePWNiUOQ9HmymUXm6Vh8kah3Tr5K14qWtyAk3xFs5aWd9Dhq0fGghdOSdAHNIACZ6%2B7QX%2Fz0%2FyHX%2FMZxwe4MoCh9jiFWC7py1zt%2FNBIgs6%2BjoVGMedsCj3i%2FdCL4QX9EEHjPG65a3f1IOBBhi1tG6M4zhncmRFeHNNEMYLG8VbYTNBlhV5CmHS%2FPztRXXxjdX%2Faf4aK5Xm%2BtUH%2FaAuIYYu7ZkocLbrX3G4HtulJidsDuRMU%2FRGpx7MWyNjUs3fGBHblWS9ghJV4IBphz

The calendar icon, which shows a small Apple logo in the lower right corner.

Sign up for new workshops

Get hands-on experience bringing the new Apple design system to your app. Dive deeper into machine learning and AI. And learn all about implementing the latest visionOS features.

Explore developer activities
https://c.apple.com/r?v=2&a=JO1XYJwWmbNCvWbplzNuaJUQ%2ByQKjhkfld3wlX%2FzieTz7ol5kfxe7GYHndPSJ79pcKpwcYGrgXT2YeOdWRybMAmKp0wP98EeEQNjqdKJ3BdDgZ6HePWNiUOQ9HmymUXm6Vh8kah3Tr5K14qWtyAk3xFs5aWd9Dhq0fGghdOSdAHNIACZ6%2B7QX%2Fz0%2FyHX%2FMZxwe4MoCh9jiFWC7py1zt%2FNBIgs6%2BjoVGMedsCj3i%2FdCL4QX9EEHjPG65a3f1IOBBhi1tG6M4zhncmRFeHNNEMYLG8VbYTNBlhV5CmHS%2FPztRXXxjdX%2Faf4aK5Xm%2BtUH%2FaAuIYYu7ZkocLbrX3G4HtulJidsDuRMU%2FRGpx7MWyNjWznx%2BtwMZ8TrhKAwtcTco%2B

A collage of screenshots of the app Sunlitt on iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple Watch. Each screen shows a graphic displaying a person's location, represented by a small white cube, and the path of the sun over it.

Let the sunshine in

Find out how the Italy-based Sunlitt team relies on Swift to follow the sun (and moon).

Read the story
https://c.apple.com/r?v=2&a=JO1XYJwWmbNCvWbplzNuaJUQ%2ByQKjhkfld3wlX%2FzieTz7ol5kfxe7GYHndPSJ79pcKpwcYGrgXT2YeOdWRybMAmKp0wP98EeEQNjqdKJ3BdDgZ6HePWNiUOQ9HmymUXm6Vh8kah3Tr5K14qWtyAk3xFs5aWd9Dhq0fGghdOSdAHNIACZ6%2B7QX%2Fz0%2FyHX%2FMZxwe4MoCh9jiFWC7py1zt%2FNBIgs6%2BjoVGMedsCj3i%2FdCL4QX9EEHjPG65a3f1IOBBhi1tG6M4zhncmRFeHNNEMYLG8VbYTNBlhV5CmHS%2FPztRXXxjdX%2Faf4aK5Xm%2BtUH%2FaAuIYYu7ZkocLbrX3G4HtulJidsDuRMU%2FRGpx7MWyNjVULoBPwwmEN6%2Buyu9MdMyj

The letters "ABC" in a variety of fonts, set in blue against a dark blue-green background. The graphic illustrates the idea of rich text experiences in SwiftUI.

Browse sample code that‘s rich and intelligent

Build rich SwiftUI text experiences
https://c.apple.com/r?v=2&a=JO1XYJwWmbNCvWbplzNuaJUQ%2ByQKjhkfld3wlX%2FzieTz7ol5kfxe7GYHndPSJ79pcKpwcYGrgXT2YeOdWRybMAmKp0wP98EeEQNjqdKJ3BdDgZ6HePWNiUOQ9HmymUXm6Vh8kah3Tr5K14qWtyAk3xFs5aWd9Dhq0fGghdOSdAHNIACZ6%2B7QX%2Fz0%2FyHX%2FMZxwe4MoCh9jiFWC7py1zt%2FNBIgs6%2BjoVGMedsCj3i%2FdCL4QX9EEHjPG65a3f1IOBBhi1tG6M4zhncmRFeHNNEMYLG8VbYTNBlhV5CmHS%2FPztRXXxjdX%2Faf4aK5Xm%2BtUH%2FaAuIYYu7ZkocLbrX3G4HtulJidsDuRMU%2FRGpx7MWyNjX13OBm5g761973YP%2BvVLov

Explore all sample code projects

View all Apple Developer Documentation updates
https://c.apple.com/r?v=2&a=JO1XYJwWmbNCvWbplzNuaJUQ%2ByQKjhkfld3wlX%2FzieTz7ol5kfxe7GYHndPSJ79pcKpwcYGrgXT2YeOdWRybMAmKp0wP98EeEQNjqdKJ3BdDgZ6HePWNiUOQ9HmymUXm6Vh8kah3Tr5K14qWtyAk3xFs5aWd9Dhq0fGghdOSdAHNIACZ6%2B7QX%2Fz0%2FyHX%2FMZxwe4MoCh9jiFWC7py1zt%2FNBIgs6%2BjoVGMedsCj3i%2FdCL4QX9EEHjPG65a3f1IOBBhi1tG6M4zhncmRFeHNNEMYLG8VbYTNBlhV5CmHS%2FPztRXXxjdX%2Faf4aK5Xm%2BtUH%2FaAuIYYu7ZkocLbrX3G4HtulJidsDuRMU%2FRGpx7MWyNjWnFlTVHJmd%2FkenL%2BL4CjQq

Share your thoughts

We’d love to hear from you. If you have suggestions for activities or stories, please let us know.

---------------------------------
          Senior Executive Taxation & Audit @ Accucorp Global Solutions | CMA Inter, BCom
        
          Third Degree Connection
        


---------------------------------

---------------------------------
If you’re interested in working on this project, submit a proposal today.

What happens after you’ve submitted a proposal?

tickets.subject: Oracle Careers Opportunities for Graduate Entry Level | Apply Now

thread.body:
Current Careers Opportunities 

By Career For Freshers


By Career For Freshers

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/oracle-careers-opportunities-graduate-entry-level-ct8jf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BAaak858GQUCui10PQ91reA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=1iF1amXuskbHU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~mdzexhkr~ve-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdzexhkr-ve&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjE4ZWNmZDc0OTk4YWI4ODYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjlkNmFmOWNjZmY1MjhiZTJkZjk5MmQ2NjUwNmEyMTdhZGUwNDk4ZDllZGM1ZTA1MiwxLDE%3D
  
An Oracle career can span industries, roles, Countries and cultures, giving you the opportunity to flourish in new roles and innovate, while blending work life in. Oracle has thrived through 40+ years of change by innovating and operating with integrity while delivering for the top companies in almost every industry. Oracle Careers Opportunities for Graduate Freshers, Oracle offers a highly competitive suite of Employee Benefits designed on the principles of parity, consistency, and affordability. The overall package includes certain core elements such as Medical, Life Insurance, access to Retirement Planning, and much more. We also encourage our employees to engage in the culture of giving back to the communities where we live and do business. Entry Level Careers Opportunities for Graduates 2025 Production Service Systems Administrator Job Responsibilities: Perform daily system operations tasks to verify system integrity and availability. Perform ongoing performance tuning, hardware/OS upgrades, and resource optimization. Implement monitoring for applications and servers, and respond to alarms in a timely manner, preventing client impact and performance degradation. Deploy physical and virtual servers. Complete assigned incident management tasks. Deploy, set up, and maintain testing environments. Provide Tier III and other support per service request, complete cyber validation and checklists to maintain cyber posture. Map standard application functionality to global/local business specifications. Complete product rollout and coordination with internal teams. Graduate Software Engineer – Mexico Functional Analyst Support Engineer Production Service Systems Administrator 1 Customer Service Analyst with English For more details information to apply Graduate Careers, Click here!
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/oracle-careers-opportunities-graduate-entry-level-ct8jf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BAaak858GQUCui10PQ91reA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=1iF1amXuskbHU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-bze0lp~mdzexhkr~ve-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdzexhkr-ve&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjE4ZWNmZDc0OTk4YWI4ODYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjlkNmFmOWNjZmY1MjhiZTJkZjk5MmQ2NjUwNmEyMTdhZGUwNDk4ZDllZGM1ZTA1MiwxLDE%3D

tickets.subject: GoodWe Named Top Inverter and Battery Manufacturer in Australia for 2025

thread.body:
GoodWe News Update

By GoodWe


By GoodWe

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/goodwe-named-top-inverter-battery-manufacturer-australia-v7lze?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BcZsDQPBDT1uyUHInh58spg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=3RXH0t_0GdbHU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~mdzj7szl~f4-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdzj7szl-f4&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjE4N2NkZDk0NjkwYTk4ODYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjkwNDBmNWZmZGQ1MDk4ZGNjODY2NWYwMDdkZjlhYWFlOTk5MWQ3NTg0MTI1Mzg5MSwxLDE%3D
  
GoodWe was awarded as the No. 2 Inverter Manufacturer and Top 5 Battery Manufacturer in Australia for 2025, according to the rankings released by Sunwiz. This reflects the strong sales performance of GoodWe inverters and batteries across the country, as well as the consumer trust the brand has built within the industry. The awards were presented earlier this year during the Smart Energy Conference and Exhibition 2025, celebrating achievements across a range of industry metrics. As GoodWe’s first overseas market, Australia has played a vital role in the company’s global journey. Over the past decade, GoodWe has significantly expanded its local operations, now encompassing dedicated sales, marketing, and technical support teams. These strategic developments reflect GoodWe’s long-term commitment to the region and its focus on delivering innovative, customer-centric energy solutions tailored to the needs of Australian customers. Dean Williamson, GoodWe Country Manager for Australia and New Zealand, commented:  “SunWiz provides the industry with crucial information and data. For us as an OEM, it’s extremely helpful because it points us in the right direction. It makes sure we’re talking to the right installers, the retailers, and provides that overall market data. Whether it’s market size or breaks it down in inverter class segments, it provides us with so much information that really enables us to focus on the right target audience moving forward.” Warwick Johnston, Managing Director of SunWiz, added: “Tracking the leading retailers is just one of the things we do in the industry, as well leading manufacturers…this year, we’re actually also awarding top battery manufacturers at the 2025 awards. For us, it’s about celebrating all those companies that have done a great job at rolling out lots of solar storage across our industry. It complements what we do with market intelligence, which is all about informing people to make good decisions so we can all move swiftly in the right direction.”
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/goodwe-named-top-inverter-battery-manufacturer-australia-v7lze?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BcZsDQPBDT1uyUHInh58spg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=3RXH0t_0GdbHU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-bze0lp~mdzj7szl~f4-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdzj7szl-f4&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjE4N2NkZDk0NjkwYTk4ODYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjkwNDBmNWZmZGQ1MDk4ZGNjODY2NWYwMDdkZjlhYWFlOTk5MWQ3NTg0MTI1Mzg5MSwxLDE%3D

tickets.subject: Fortanix Monthly Newsletter July 2025

thread.body:
Fortanix Monthly Newsletter

By Fortanix


By Fortanix

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/fortanix-monthly-newsletter-july-2025-fortanix-7xihc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3B2V8TEeRwT6q8rMOxlO1C9Q%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=2VgfSHeT50bXU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~mdzpnaep~f9-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdzpnaep-f9&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjI4NmNmZDc0NjlmYWE4YjYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjk5NDVkNWVlYzc3NWE2YTk5NTJjNTNlNDQ1NWMxNmIxNzk3MmVhYmNkMjJmMjEzNSwxLDE%3D
  
Last month, we launched PQC Central, a new Fortanix Key Insight feature that helps assess quantum risk, score crypto readiness, and guide migration—no hardware changes or key management disruption needed. Read the full press release here.  This month, we’re continuing the PQC readiness focus with blogs, webinars, and expert insights. If you're preparing for crypto-agility and tracking upcoming mandates like the EU’s 2026 deadline, stay tuned.  LinkedIn Live Session     Inside Black Hat: Briefing with Fortanix CEO Anand Kashyap 📌 Tune in for an exclusive, off-the-record intelligence briefing built for CISOs and security leaders on Thursday, August 7, at 11:00 AM PST. This event will feature: ✅ Fortanix CEO, Anand Kashyap’s candid take on the evolving threat landscape and modern data security  ✅ A walk of the Black Hat floor to analyze vendor messaging in real time  ✅ Anand’s perspective on what’s being said (and what’s being missed)  ✅ Actionable insights and takeaways from the event, including a conversation with a respected voice in the security space (guest to be announced)    👉Register here Webcast Webcast: Act Now To Secure Your Agency's Data from Quantum Threats Act Now to Secure Your Agency's Data from Quantum Threats The shift to post-quantum cryptography (PQC) is underway. EO 14144 calls for urgent action, and the risk of "harvest now, decrypt later" is real.  Join cybersecurity leaders as they discuss what PQC readiness means for federal agencies and how to begin building your roadmap.  Learn how post-quantum cryptography will shape the future of federal cybersecurity.  Gain actionable insights for integrating PQC readiness into your security strategy.  Discover how to build a tailored PQC roadmap for your agency.    Featured Speakers: 🔹 Dr. Matthew McFadden, VP Cyber & Division CISO, GDIT  🔹 Manish Bhaskar, VP Product Management, Fortanix    📅 August 12, 2025  🕑 2:00pm ET | 11:00am PT  📍 Online | 60 mins  👉More Details: Act Now to Secure Your Agency's Data From Quantum Threats    On-Demand Webcast  Your Data is Not Safe! Quantum Readiness is Urgent Your Data Is Not Safe! Quantum Readiness is Urgent As governments and regulators push for post-quantum cryptography (PQC) readiness by 2026, the time to act is now. Watch this webcast as Fortanix and cybersecurity leaders from Citi, PenFed Credit Union, and Futuriom have an exclusive discussion on how large enterprises assess their cryptographic risk and build realistic PQC roadmaps.  Highlights:  How to discover and assess quantum-vulnerable encryption in your environment  Lessons from real-world PQC readiness projects at scale  Practical steps to start your transition without overhauling your infrastructure  Insights on cryptographic policy, IT coordination, and regulatory timelines  Featured Speakers:  Sudha E Iyer , Chief Cybersecurity Architect, Citi   David Chapman , Director of Identity Access Management, PenFed Credit Union   Dr. Richard Searle , Chief AI Officer, Fortanix  Craig Matsumoto , Contributing Analyst, Futuriom   👉Watch the video here  eBook Spotlight  The Ultimate Guide for Post Quantum Readiness The Ultimate Guide for Post-Quantum Readiness  Learn what post-quantum cryptography means for your business, why acting now matters, and how to begin the shift with a structured, four-step plan. It also covers how tools like Fortanix Key Insight help make this transition more manageable.  👉 Download the eBook Upcoming Events   Upcoming Events Black Hat USA 2025: 02 – 07, August  Top minds in cybersecurity meet in Vegas for deep technical sessions and a walk through the real-world threat landscape. Expect sharp perspectives, bold vendor claims, and big security questions.  👉Event link »   The Future of Privacy and Security: 13 August  Australia’s most relevant privacy and security summit addresses upcoming reforms, breach trends, and what security leadership needs to prepare for now. An essential stop for CISOs and privacy pros.  👉Event link »  Carahsoft Billington Annual Cybersecurity Summit: 09 –12, September  The top U.S. public sector security event returns with agency leaders, mission-first strategies, and real talk on critical infrastructure threats. Expect policy meets practice.  👉Event link »  Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit 2025: 22 – 24, September  Gartner’s flagship summit in the UK brings clarity on AI, risk, and security operations. Insight-packed sessions tailored for CISOs shaping their next roadmap.  👉Event link »  Transformational CISO Assembly Europe: 04 – 05,  November  Invite-only. Strategy-heavy. This is where Europe’s leading CISOs cut through the noise and solve enterprise-scale challenges, from cloud controls to leadership priorities.  👉Event link »  Case Study   How Blackbaud Can Strengthen Data Security and Compiance with Fortanix Key Insight How Blackbaud Can Strengthen Data Security and Compliance with Fortanix Key Insight for Automated Key Management and Risk Assessment   What’s Inside: This case study explains how Fortanix proposed Key Insight to help Blackbaud address encryption key sprawl, manual reporting challenges, and compliance requirements across AWS, Azure, and on-premises environments. It describes how Key Insight can provide agentless key discovery, ongoing cryptographic assessments, and visibility into quantum vulnerabilities. The case outlines how the solution could save Blackbaud significant time, improve audit readiness, and strengthen encryption controls.  👉Read Here News & Blogs  Fortanix Blogs Achieving Zero Trust Architecture with Cloud HSM | Read  What Are the Challenges of Key Management (with Solutions) | Read  What Modern Data Security Management Looks Like | Read  Difference Between BYOE & BYOK in Data Security Space | Read  3 Overlooked Areas That Impact Key Management Strategy | Read  CISO roundtable: Implementing Tokenization for Resilient Data Security | Read  How to Achieve Enterprise Data Security with KMS & HSM | Read  How Data-Centric Security Solutions for Enterprise Help Achieve Compliance | Read  How to Choose the Best Data Security Company | Read  Why Data Security in the Cloud Needs to Evolve for Post Quantum Threats | Read  Simplify Your Path to PCI DSS 4.0 Compliance | Read  How Top CISOs Leverage Tokenization for Data Security | Read  How Cloud HSM Simplifies Key Management for Enterprises | Read  Is CSPM Security Enough to Secure Your Data in Cloud | Read 
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/fortanix-monthly-newsletter-july-2025-fortanix-7xihc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3B2V8TEeRwT6q8rMOxlO1C9Q%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=2VgfSHeT50bXU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-bze0lp~mdzpnaep~f9-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdzpnaep-f9&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjI4NmNmZDc0NjlmYWE4YjYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjk5NDVkNWVlYzc3NWE2YTk5NTJjNTNlNDQ1NWMxNmIxNzk3MmVhYmNkMjJmMjEzNSwxLDE%3D

tickets.subject: A Guide to Web Accessibility: Making Sites for Everybody

thread.body:
Tech Blogs at Times Internet

By Times Internet


By Times Internet

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/guide-web-accessibility-making-sites-everybody-timesinternet-vzvmc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3B3KFOXgFKRZuyDy2QQiQ2SA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=2iCq7zGyZZbXU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~mdzrsg63~wk-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdzrsg63-wk&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjM4ZGM5ZDg0MzlhYTk4ZjYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmVhNGFlOGNmODU3ZWZlZDNjNzFlODA2NzA3M2IyNmI5ZTNjMjhlOTNkNzZiNzU1MiwxLDE%3D
  
Authored By: Niraj Tiwari & Daya Yadav Co-Authored By: Ashish Jaiswal Introduction: What is Web Accessibility? Imagine entering a building with no ramps, narrow doors, and signs only in tiny, unreadable fonts. That's how many people experience the web when websites aren't accessible. Here's something that might surprise you: when we talk about web accessibility, we're not just talking about compliance checkboxes or legal requirements. We're talking about opening digital doors for millions of people who navigate the web differently than you might expect. Web accessibility means making websites usable for everyone, including people with disabilities such as visual, auditory, motor, or cognitive impairments. With over 1 billion people worldwide living with disabilities (15% of the global population), accessibility isn't just a nice-to-have; it's essential for inclusive digital experiences. Why Should You Care? Legal Requirements: Global laws mandate accessibility. Key regulations include the ADA (USA), Section 508 (USA), the EU Web Accessibility Directive, AODA (Canada), and India's Rights of Persons with Disabilities (RPWD) Act. Business Impact: The disability market represents $13 trillion in annual disposable income globally SEO Benefits: Accessible sites rank better in search engines Better UX for Everyone: Captions help in noisy environments, and high contrast helps in bright sunlight Spotlight on India: Web Accessibility Regulations India has a strong legal framework, making web accessibility a mandatory requirement for both government and private entities. The Rights of Persons with Disabilities (RPWD) Act, 2016: This is the primary law. It mandates that all organizations, public and private, must provide accessible services, including websites and mobile apps. It treats digital accessibility as a fundamental right. Guidelines for Indian Government Websites (GIGW): While initially for government sites, GIGW is the de facto standard for all. It requires compliance with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.0 Level AA. Enforcement: Non-compliance with the RPWD Act can result in penalties. The law empowers citizens to file complaints against inaccessible digital properties, making compliance a critical business need. 📚 Understanding Accessibility  Let's say a person broke his arm and can't use a mouse. Or a person is colorblind. Or maybe a person prefers listening to articles instead of reading them. A good website makes sure all these people can still use it easily. Accessibility means: You don't need a mouse; just a keyboard is enough If you can't see pictures, there's text explaining what they are If you use a screen reader, it reads out everything properly The website doesn't rely only on colors to explain things Content is easy to understand, even if you have dyslexia or ADHD Common Misconception: "Accessible websites are ugly or boring." Actually, many beautiful, modern websites are fully accessible, you just don't notice because they work so well! 🏗️ The Building Analogy: Accessibility is a Ramp Think of a website like a building. Now imagine: Ramps for wheelchairs = keyboard navigation Braille signs or audio instructions = screen reader support Bright lighting and readable signs = good color contrast and clear text Clear wayfinding = logical navigation and consistent layouts Automatic doors = skip links and shortcuts Web accessibility is the digital equivalent of making sure everyone can enter the building, move around, and get the information they need. 📋 Breaking Down WCAG 2.2 (The Golden Rules of Accessibility) The Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) are the global recipe book for making accessible websites. They're built on four main principles, just remember the acronym POUR: 1. Perceivable: Can users see or hear your content? Text Alternatives - Provide alt text for images, icons, and non-text content. Time-Based Media - Add captions or transcripts for videos and audio. Adaptability - Content should be accessible in different formats (e.g., readable without CSS). Distinguishable - Use high-contrast colors, allow text resizing, and avoid using color alone to convey meaning. 2. Operable: Can users navigate your site? Keyboard Accessibility - The website should be fully usable with a keyboard (no mouse required). Enough Time - Users should have enough time to read and use content (e.g., avoid automatic timeouts or allow extensions). Seizure Prevention - Avoid flashing content that can cause seizures. Navigation Assistance - Provide clear navigation, headings, and consistent structure. Input Modalities - Make it easy to interact using different devices (e.g., touchscreen, speech input). 3. Understandable: Can users make sense of your content? Readable Content - Use simple language, clear instructions, and define difficult words. Predictable Behavior - Navigation and layout should be consistent across all pages. Input Assistance - Help users avoid and correct mistakes (e.g., form validation and clear error messages). 4. Robust: Does your site work with different tools? Compatible with Assistive Technologies - Ensure that screen readers and other tools can read and interact with the website. Use Standard HTML & ARIA Roles - Follow proper coding standards so that assistive technologies can understand the content correctly. WCAG  has three levels: Note: While many regulations point to WCAG 2.0 or 2.1, adhering to the latest version, WCAG 2.2, is the best practice to ensure future compliance and a better user experience. It adds new criteria like ensuring help features are consistently located and that focused elements are never completely hidden. 💼 The Business Case Why Invest in Accessibility? Avoid Legal Risk:  In India, penalties under the RPWD Act can be substantial. Expand Your Market: Improve SEO: Google rewards accessible sites with better rankings (ET saw organic traffic increase after accessibility improvements) Reduce Support Costs: Accessible interfaces reduce customer service inquiries Quick Wins for Your Team Add accessibility checkpoints to your project timeline Budget 15-20% of development time for accessibility Use this Accessibility Requirements Template for project briefs 💻 For Developers: Technical Deep Dive What is ARIA? ARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications) is a set of special attributes that help make websites and web apps more accessible to people who use screen readers or assistive technologies. Modern websites often use dynamic elements like pop-ups, dropdown menus, sliders, and interactive forms. These elements can be difficult for people with disabilities to use. ARIA helps by adding extra information to these elements, making them readable and interactive for assistive technologies like screen readers. Quick Start Checklist Common ARIA Pitfalls and Solutions Overusing ARIA: First rule of ARIA: Don't use ARIA if native HTML works. Native elements have built-in keyboard accessibility, roles, and states, which you would have to manually replicate with ARIA, increasing complexity and the risk of errors. Missing Labels: Every form input needs a label or aria-label Focus Management: When modals open, focus should move to them Live Regions: Use aria-live for dynamic content updates Performance Considerations Lazy load images, but ensure alt text loads immediately ARIA live regions can impact performance if overused Semantic HTML is faster than ARIA-enhanced divs 📊 Case Study: Economic Times' Accessibility Transformation The Economic Times (ET) demonstrates how major news sites can achieve excellent accessibility scores. Here's their journey: Before Optimization Scores Home Page: 69% (Desktop) / 77% (Mobile) Article Pages: 67% (Desktop) / 84% (Mobile) Live Blogs: 90% (Desktop) / 93% (Mobile) After Fixes Scores Home Page: 91% (Desktop) / 91% (Mobile) Article Pages: 93% (Desktop) / 91% (Mobile) Company Page: 100% (Desktop) / 100% (Mobile) ✨ Key Issues ET Addressed Color Contrast: Fixed insufficient contrast ratios between text and backgrounds Touch Targets: Increased menu bar touch target sizes for mobile users HTML Structure: Removed invalid div/span elements from ul/li structures Alt Text: Added descriptions for all images (including ad-served content) Heading Hierarchy: Fixed sequential ordering of h1, h2, h3 elements Lessons from ET's Journey: Start with Quick Wins: Company pages reached 100% by fixing issues Mobile Matters: Mobile scores often differ from desktop - test both Dynamic Content Challenge: Ad widgets and dynamic content need special attention Consistency Pays: Fixing menu bars site-wide improved scores across all pages ✅ Success Story Economic Times (ET): Transformed accessibility scores from 67-97% to 91-100% across all page types. ET’s Company page achieved a perfect 100% score on both desktop and mobile, proving news sites can be fully accessible. Added keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, and focus indicators. ❌ Common Mistakes We See Images without alt text: Screen readers announce "image" with no context (ET fixed this even for ad-served images) Placeholder text as labels: Disappears when typing, confusing for users with memory issues Custom dropdowns built with divs: Break keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements Auto-playing videos: Interfere with screen readers and can trigger sensory issues Infinite scroll: Traps keyboard users and makes footer content unreachable Poor contrast ratios: ET found this was their #1 issue across all page types Small touch targets: Especially problematic on mobile menu bars 🧠 Cognitive Accessibility: Often Overlooked but Critical Design for Cognitive Differences Clear Language: Avoid jargon, use short sentences Consistent Navigation: Keep menus in the same place White Space: Don't overwhelm with too much information Progress Indicators: Show where users are in multi-step processes Error Recovery: Allow users to go back and fix mistakes easily Real Example 🛠️ Tools and Resources Testing Tools WAVE: Visual feedback about accessibility axe DevTools: Browser extension for developers Lighthouse: Built into Chrome DevTools Screen Readers for Testing Windows: NVDA (free), JAWS Mac: VoiceOver (built-in) Mobile: TalkBack (Android), VoiceOver (iOS) 📝 Key Takeaways  Accessibility means making your website usable for everyone, including 15% of the population with disabilities Following accessibility guidelines is legally required in many jurisdictions Use the POUR principles: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust Start with semantic HTML, good color contrast, and keyboard navigation Test with real assistive technologies and real users Accessibility improves SEO, reduces support costs, and expands your market 🚀 Your Next Steps If You're a Developer Audit: Run axe DevTools on your current project (ET used Lighthouse for all audits) Fix: Start with critical issues (missing alt text, keyboard traps, contrast ratios) Automate: Add accessibility tests to your CI/CD pipeline Practice: Build your next component with an accessibility-first approach Remember: Perfect accessibility is impossible, but every improvement helps real people. Economic Times improved from 67% to 93%+ scores - your site can too! Want to work on products that impact millions and push the bar every day? Join us and #TakeUsToTheNextLevel: https://timesinternet.in/careers Credits Puneet Kukreja, Priyabrata Bera, Harsh Kumar Saini, Abhinav Singal, Amit Kumar Srivastava, Kajal Pal, Sudhir Kumar References https://www.w3.org/WAI/planning/statements/ https://guidelines.india.gov.in/tools-and-resources/#accessibility https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/ https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/  https://wave.webaim.org/  https://www.deque.com/axe/devtools/  https://developers.google.com/web/tools/lighthouse  https://www.nvaccess.org/  https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/  https://www.weforum.org/stories/2023/12/driving-disability-inclusion-is-more-than-a-moral-imperative-it-s-a-business-one/

tickets.subject: The world’s toughest exam

thread.body:
The Economist’s week ahead

By The Economist


By The Economist

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/worlds-toughest-exam-the-economist-sxpye?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BP1mkYBy7RrqQIiWqCRYpFA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=11yvrvksuUbXU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~mdztxlyy~rt-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdztxlyy-rt&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjM4OWNkZDc0MjkxYTg4YTYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjgxNzVkNGQ5ZmY0ZThkZDEwZDc2ZDk3NWIxODZlNzJkNzA5ODY2YjM1NzJjODcyNSwxLDE%3D
  
Sign up for free here to receive this newsletter in your inbox every Saturday. You’ll also get our weekly newsletter with an exclusive introduction from our editor-in-chief. Every January our editors start to think about The Economist’s summer issue. Our deeply reported pieces take months to come together, but we also want them to feel timely when they come out. Sometimes, timing works in our favour. Liam Taylor, who wrote a jaw-dropping story about illegal gold-miners who were blockaded underground for months in South Africa, arrived at the mouth of the shaft just as the official rescue attempt was being mounted.  We have three more 1843 long reads for you to enjoy this week. First, discover the world’s toughest exam. Are you brainy enough to get a job on the Indian railways? (The piece includes an interactive quiz so you can find out.) You can also read the story of Omer Shem Tov, who was kidnapped by Hamas and spent 500 days as a hostage. And get to know William Woods, who was sent to a psychiatric institution for insisting that his identity had been stolen. But was he telling the truth all along? Jonathan Beckman, Editor, 1843  Editor's picks Would you pass the world’s toughest exam?  Thirty million Indians want a job on the railways, but a fiendish general-knowledge test stands in their way Dying for gold: who killed the miners of Buffelsfontein? South Africa’s government blockaded hundreds underground. The results were deadly I spent 500 days as a hostage of Hamas Omer Shem Tov was kidnapped on October 7th. He survived by becoming his captors’ cook One William Woods was telling the truth. The other was living his life How a decades-long deception saw an innocent man end up in jail Go beyond the headlines Get unlimited digital access to The Economist by subscribing today.
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/worlds-toughest-exam-the-economist-sxpye?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BP1mkYBy7RrqQIiWqCRYpFA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=11yvrvksuUbXU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-bze0lp~mdztxlyy~rt-null-null&eid=bze0lp-mdztxlyy-rt&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYjM4OWNkZDc0MjkxYTg4YTYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjgxNzVkNGQ5ZmY0ZThkZDEwZDc2ZDk3NWIxODZlNzJkNzA5ODY2YjM1NzJjODcyNSwxLDE%3D

thread.body:
************************************************************************************************************************
Hi ASIF, 

Great to see you in Tasker! Now that you're set up, let's make sure you have everything you need to succeed in the beta.
************************************************************************************************************************

**********************
Your Beta Headquarters
**********************

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Join our Reddit community - this is where the beta community hangs out:
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

r/TaskerAl ( https://www.reddit.com/r/TaskerAI/ )

*****************
Here you'll find:
*****************

Template

Ready-made task recipes you can use immediately

Support

E-mail support@mail.tasker.ai for help from our team

Feature voting

Help us decide what to build next

Tips & tricks

Learn from power users

*******
Help Us
*******

******************
Make Tasker Better
******************

Your experience matters. here's how to share feedback:

*
Quick feedback: Hit reply to any of our emails
* Bug reports: Post in r/TaskerAl with the "Bug" flair
* Feature requests: Use the "Feature Request" flair in Reddit
* General thoughts: Comment on our weekly feedback threads

***************
Spread the word
***************

Love what you're seeing? We'd appreciate you sharing Tasker with friends who might benefit... That is once we fully launch. We'll keep you in the loop with how to help us stick the launch with referrals rewards, prizes and more.

************
What's Next?
************

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Keep an eye out for our next email in a few days - we'll share some power user tips to help you get the most out of Tasker.  Questions? Just hit reply.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Happy automating!

thread.body:
Current Careers Opportunities 

By Career For Freshers


By Career For Freshers

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/off-campus-tesla-graduate-entry-level-jobs-2025-career-for-freshers-plc5f?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3Bh1cpk09dQQmbSdPwOXUIZg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=0T3kUrXSZKcbU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~me00d8bn~lx-null-null&eid=bze0lp-me00d8bn-lx&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYmM4OGNjZDg0MjllYWE4YTYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjgyNDBkMGRmODU2M2ZmZDFmMzliODE5ODRiYjBhYTY4YjU1ODZhNzZhOWE3MTYyMiwxLDE%3D
  
Tesla, Inc. is an American multinational automotive and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas. Tesla designs and manufactures electric  vehicles, battery energy storage from home to grid-scale, solar panels and solar roof tiles, and related products and services. Tesla Careers Opportunities for Graduate Entry Level Fresher, Internship and Entry Level role in a various domain such as Engineering, Technology, Digital Technology, Automation,  Software, Business Operation, seals and Marketing, services and many more at USA, and other countries 2024 We are looking for a bright and eager professional who is seeking the opportunity of a lifetime and is excited to be a part of our mission to accelerate the world’s transition to sustainable energy. This exciting career opportunity will support Tesla’s Global Team Tesla Entry Level Careers Opportunities In 2025 Customer Experience Specialist, Vehicle Handover (Full Time) – UK Job Description: Welcome customers and provide an exceptional handover experience. Conduct key handovers, ensuring all documentation is completed. Offer guidance on vehicle features and answer customer queries. Maintain high vehicle presentation standards at collection sites. Assist in moving vehicles to designated areas. Manage appointment flow to optimise efficiency and reduce wait times. Support Tesla Direct deliveries when required. Coordinate with other members of the Tesla team to provide a seamless and exceptional customer experience. Always maintain a positive and professional attitude, providing an excellent customer experience that is reflective of Tesla’s values. Senior Operations Specialist (Accounts Receivable), Leasing – UK Seasonal Tesla Advisor We are seeking a candidate with both exceptional customer service skills and a genuine commitment to the cause of sustainable energy. The Seasonal Tesla Advisor position represents a unique chance to showcase your skills and talents within Tesla. Associate Energy Systems Technical Support Engineer – Australia Manufacturing Equipment Engineer, Remanufacturing Integration Engineer, Factory Software We are seeking a highly motivated Integration Engineer to join our Factory  Software Team. In this role, you will be responsible for integrating new manufacturing lines with our homegrown Factory, Warehouse, and ERP systems and providing ongoing support to ensure their smooth daily operation. For more details information to apply Tesla Graduate Careers, Click here!
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/off-campus-tesla-graduate-entry-level-jobs-2025-career-for-freshers-plc5f?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3Bh1cpk09dQQmbSdPwOXUIZg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=0T3kUrXSZKcbU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-bze0lp~me00d8bn~lx-null-null&eid=bze0lp-me00d8bn-lx&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYmM4OGNjZDg0MjllYWE4YTYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZjgyNDBkMGRmODU2M2ZmZDFmMzliODE5ODRiYjBhYTY4YjU1ODZhNzZhOWE3MTYyMiwxLDE%3D

tickets.subject: Johnson & Johnson Careers Opportunities

thread.body:
Current Careers Opportunities 

By Career For Freshers


By Career For Freshers

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/johnson-careers-opportunities-career-for-freshers-onmje?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3Bnk8w6kg2S3KG7WkeV9W7Xg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=2zjEwGSeGFcbU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~me02i9i7~zu-null-null&eid=bze0lp-me02i9i7-zu&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYmQ4ZmM2ZDE0MzlmYWI4OTYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmI2NmRmMWQzZmI3NDhjZWVmMzU5NTQxMzcyZTFhNDQ0YTJmNGUyZDRhYjVkZTIxYywxLDE%3D
  
At Johnson & Johnson, the largest healthcare company in the world, Johnson & Johnson Global Services, the global shared services organization supporting the businesses of Johnson & Johnson, performs select functional work in a consistent manner across regions and sectors using simplified, standardized end-to-end processes and state-of-the-art technology. Johnson & Johnson J&J Careers Opportunities for  Graduate Freshers, Entry Level, Mid Level in various domain such as Technology, Engineering, Healthcare, Operation, Business, Customer services Calling all big thinkers and world-changers who want to drive their careers forward across more than 250 leading businesses in pharmaceutical, and medical technology. If you have the talent and desire to touch the world, Johnson & Johnson has the career opportunities to help make it happen. Johnson & Johnson Careers Opportunities for Entry Level Product Specialist – Saudi Arabia This role is for proactive hiring to build a robust talent pipeline for future openings across Saudi Arabia. If you are passionate about improving patients’ lives within Immunology, Oncology/Hematology, Neuroscience & PAH, we invite you to submit your application. By applying here, you will be considered for future roles that align with your skills and expertise. The main task of a Product Specialist is to represent Johnson & Johnson in front of healthcare professionals, making sure our products are used based on their own merit, and according to their clinical indications, while respecting all J&J Credo obligations. PS promotes registered products within registered indications in order to increase Johnson & Johnson’s products access in collaboration with other functions. This takes place by detailing our product’s value in order to serve more eligible patients. Qualifications Graduate of medical sciences background (Medical- Dentistry-Veterinary-Pharmacy) 0-3 years of Pharmaceutical experience Digital knowledge is an advantage Location: Riyadh, Jeddah, Dammam, Abha Language: Fluency in English Accountant, Global Fixed Asset – Philippines A fixed asset accountant performs various functions in ensuring effective maintenance of an organization’s fixed assets and depreciation schedules. Analyst, Global Data Manager – US The Analyst, Global Data Manager (GDM) is a professional individual contributor role at entry level that will provide oversight and accountability and/or execute data management activities and/or perform scientific data review for one or more trials of low to moderate complexity. QA Engineer – Netherlands As the Quality Assurance Engineer, you will play a crucial role in ensuring that our operations meet the highest standards of quality and compliance. You will have the opportunity to work with a team of exceptional professionals and contribute to the success of our goals. For more details information to apply Johnson & Johnson Graduate Careers, Click here!
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/johnson-careers-opportunities-career-for-freshers-onmje?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3Bnk8w6kg2S3KG7WkeV9W7Xg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=2zjEwGSeGFcbU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-bze0lp~me02i9i7~zu-null-null&eid=bze0lp-me02i9i7-zu&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYmQ4ZmM2ZDE0MzlmYWI4OTYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmI2NmRmMWQzZmI3NDhjZWVmMzU5NTQxMzcyZTFhNDQ0YTJmNGUyZDRhYjVkZTIxYywxLDE%3D

tickets.subject: Don’t Let Your PTO (Or Credit Card Points) Go To Waste

thread.body:
Forbes Advisor’s Weekly Brief

By Forbes Advisor


By Forbes Advisor

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/dont-let-your-pto-credit-card-points-go-waste-forbes-advisor-tnq1e?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BW%2BqBCwGdThibelE1OhuMbg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=2zjEwGSeGFcbU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-bze0lp~me02i9i7~rd-null-null&eid=bze0lp-me02i9i7-rd&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYmQ4ZmM2ZDE0Mjk4YTc4NjYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmI5NzFlMmNjZDY1YmI5Y2M2NDg4YzhjMGE0OGE1OTMwOTA5Zjc0MmU3MTM4MmI2MCwxLDE%3D
  
A Note From Becky: Hello, and welcome to Forbes Advisor’s Weekly Brief, where we dive into the realities of consumer finance and empower you with knowledge to help make your financial journey easier. Let me guess: You meant to plan your 2025 travel months ago. But somehow, summer’s more than halfway over, your PTO days are piling up and flight prices are…less than ideal.  While more notice might be ideal, you can still pull together an amazing trip this year with a few smart moves. Whether you’re dreaming of a sunny beach, city-hopping abroad or just a long weekend somewhere more interesting than your living room, your points can give you options you’ll actually like. You’ve worked hard for those vacation days (and your credit card points). Let’s make them work for you, even if you feel like you’re arriving late to the booking game. From our partner: Managing money is one of the most time-consuming responsibilities for small business owners, as they often juggle unpredictable income, recurring expenses and tight deadlines. Learn how platforms like QuickBooks can help give financial clarity and increase cash flow visibility here. Catch you in the lounge, Becky Pokora Senior Staff Writer You Have Access To More Flights Than You Think Gone are the days where your miles only gave you access to flights on the named airline. Most airlines have partnerships or alliances with a dozen or more other airlines and their websites and apps finally make it easy to search and book the full spectrum of destinations, routing options and experiences.  But credit card flexible points are the real game-changer. Most of these travel rewards cards partner with 10 or more programs—multiply that across each partner’s own network and your options grow exponentially.  The best part of flexible points, though, is you can hold onto them until you need them—and only initiate a transfer when you have an exact need at hand. That’s far more versatile than being locked into a single airline. If you have a credit card that allows point transfers, login to your account and re-read the list of participating airlines and hotels; a few banks have added airlines this year. We’re Entering Bargain Season For once, procrastination may actually be working in your favor. The latter part of the year is often a sweet spot for award travel, especially if you’re flexible. October through mid-December means fewer travelers, with kids back in school but before the holiday season picks up. That lull means less competition for award seats and a high shot of finding something great. And if you’re in the privileged position of being able to pack up and go anytime—I’m jealous. Last minute wanderlust is especially rewarding. August award flights are more plentiful than usual as airlines with unsold tickets open up redemptions in order to offload miles from their books. So if business class from San Francisco to Tokyo catches your eye, using availability to inspire where to go can be a fantastic way to score a good redemption. Big Welcome Offers Are Out There One-time welcome bonuses are one of the best ways to build (or rebuild) your mileage balance. Banks are being more selective with who gets the best sign-up offers in 2025, but well-qualified applicants will find offers worth $1,000 or more in travel right now. Earning these bonuses typically requires spending a specified amount within a few months of receiving the card. Even if you won’t earn a bonus in time for fall travel, anticipate future needs. If you have upcoming expenses like back-to-school shopping, car repairs or even holiday flights, timing your application to line up with those purchases is a strategic way to hit your spending requirements without too much effort. Related Stories: Best Travel Credit Cards 16 Best Places To Travel in 2025 Using Credit Card Rewards Credit Card Transfer Partners: A Comparison of the Different Points Programs
Keep reading on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/dont-let-your-pto-credit-card-points-go-waste-forbes-advisor-tnq1e?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_01%3BW%2BqBCwGdThibelE1OhuMbg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFArWM_SCnWaw&midSig=2zjEwGSeGFcbU1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_01-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-bze0lp~me02i9i7~rd-null-null&eid=bze0lp-me02i9i7-rd&otpToken=MTUwNzFhZTUxNDI2Y2RjMmI1MjQwNGVkNDMxYWUzYmQ4ZmM2ZDE0Mjk4YTc4NjYxNzRjMzAzNjY0YjVlNTRmYmY0ZDRkZmI5NzFlMmNjZDY1YmI5Y2M2NDg4YzhjMGE0OGE1OTMwOTA5Zjc0MmU3MTM4MmI2MCwxLDE%3D
  
      

----------------------------------------
